# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Node.js Express application. Use the official Node.js base image and install dependencies with npm install. The app listens on port 3000. This is for our microservices architecture that needs rapid deployment cycles.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

# Use the official Node.js runtime as a parent image
FROM node:14

# Set work directory in Docker file 
WORKDIR /usr/src/app

# Copy package and package lock files into docker container at build time, this is for cache purposes to avoid re-installing dependencies each run of the app.  
COPY package*.json ./

# Install all necessary packages defined in requirements (in a file named "package-lock").  This will also install any missing or conflicting versions that were previously installed by npm during build time, and lock them into place for this step to use as cache dependencies when the next run of app is made.  
RUN npm install --silent -y && \
    rm package*.json
    
# Copy all files from current directory (except .gitignore) in Dockerfile context – ie., your source code into container at build time, this will be the place where you'll have access to any local development tools and dependencies.  
COPY . ./

EXPOSE 3000
# Expose port for communication with other services (default is http) if needed by app in production environment or use a different one like https, depending on your needs    
ENTRYPOINT ["node", "app.js"] # Run the Node application when container starts up
